brief_notes: Strong DTI prediction paper with two clear contributions: a 100x larger dataset (SCOPE) and a 3D structure-aware model. The semi-inductive and fully inductive evaluation paradigms are well-motivated and address real limitations of existing benchmarks. Ablation study is informative, showing bilinear attention as the most important component. Experimental validation (76% hit rate in top 50) adds credibility. Weaknesses: 13 source databases listed but not all named, bias filtering criteria could be more precisely defined, binary classification ignores binding affinity magnitude, limited references (~12), and the 76% validation rate lacks comparison to random baseline enrichment. The fully inductive AUROC of 0.878 is impressive but needs more analysis of what types of interactions it fails on.
